profile.name: Lind
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; drives planning agenda and keeps track of most account details and paperwork.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Upper-intermediate: comfortable with brokerage vs. retirement accounts, budgeting, and basic tax concepts; asks for clarity on alternatives/private markets and distribution rules.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate and structured: willing to stay invested if the plan is clear, but prefers guardrails (rebalancing bands, cash buffer) and gets uneasy with opaque holdings or sudden big shifts.
profile.communication_style: Direct and agenda-first; uses exact figures when she has them and corrects small inaccuracies. When unsure, hedges with "I think"/"roughly" and asks to confirm. Likes a recap and action list.
emotional_tendencies: relief when tradeoffs are spelled out
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical with checkpoints: wants options, assumptions, and a recommendation; prefers to review once, then decide quickly if it aligns with retirement timing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: expects transparency on fees, taxes, and what is known vs. estimated; trust rises when the advisor repeats numbers accurately.
hidden_concerns: whether retiring around 68 is realistic without cutting lifestyle
tax impact of changes in the taxable brokerage and alternatives
not having any protection policies documented and what that could mean

profile.name: Michael
profile.role_in_household: Retired spouse; focuses on lifestyle comfort and downside protection, weighs in on big decisions and timing.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Basic-to-intermediate: understands mortgage payment, cash cushion, and broad account buckets; mixes up terms (balance vs. payment, gross vs. net) and prefers plain-English explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative in feel: prefers visible safety (cash, paying down debt) and is skeptical of complex investments; can accept moderate risk if framed as supporting steady retirement income and avoiding forced selling.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers ("about," "around") and asks clarifying questions midstream. Often looks to Linda to confirm specifics and may defer on technical details.
emotional_tendencies: discomfort when discussions get too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking: wants to hear the advisor’s recommendation and Linda’s view, then agrees once he feels the downside is covered and the plan is simple to follow.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded initially: trust builds with patience, minimal jargon, and clear explanations of why each step matters.
internal_biases: mental accounting (cash feels untouchable; investments feel risky)
hidden_concerns: outliving assets or needing to sell investments in a downturn
keeping the mortgage manageable through retirement
whether their income sources are being interpreted correctly (gross vs. net confusion)
